description: Opens a persistent WebSocket connection that streams real-time telemetry readings from IoT devices. The server pushes JSON messages as they arrive from the MQTT ingestion bridge. Use the optional device_id query parameter to subscribe to a single device; omit it to receive the broadcast feed for all devices. The connection remains open until either side closes it.

queryParameters.name: device_id
queryParameters.description: Filter the stream to a single device. When set, only telemetry for this device ID is pushed. Omit for the full broadcast feed.

clientToServer.description: The client can optionally send text frames to keep the connection alive. The server ignores the content but resets the idle timeout on any received frame.

messageSchema.description: Any text frame (typically "ping" or empty string).

serverToClient.description: The server pushes JSON-encoded telemetry readings. Each message represents a single metric value captured at a point in time for a specific device.

metric.description: Metric name (temperature, humidity, voltage, cpu_load, etc.).

errors.description: Sent when the device_id query parameter references a device that does not exist.
Sent when the backend MQTT bridge is unavailable or encounters an unrecoverable error.
Sent when the server is shutting down gracefully (e.g. during deployment).

connectionLifecycle.handshake: Standard HTTP upgrade to WebSocket at the given path. No authentication headers required in development; production requires a valid session cookie or Bearer token in the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header.
connectionLifecycle.heartbeat: The server sends a WebSocket ping frame every 30 seconds. If the client does not respond with a pong within 10 seconds, the connection is closed with code 1001.
connectionLifecycle.reconnection: Clients should implement exponential backoff reconnection (initial delay 1s, max 30s). The server does not buffer missed messages during disconnection.
connectionLifecycle.idleTimeout: Connections idle for more than 60 seconds (no frames in either direction) are closed by the server.

description: Opens a persistent WebSocket connection that streams real-time updates for all dashboard widgets. Messages arrive as typed envelopes with a discriminator field (type) and a data payload. The frontend dashboard hook dispatches each envelope to the appropriate widget reducer. This single connection replaces polling for KPIs, pipeline status, data streams, alerts, and anomaly counts.

clientToServer.description: The client can send text frames to keep the connection alive. No structured commands are currently supported.

messageSchema.description: Any text frame (typically "ping" or empty string).

serverToClient.description: The server pushes typed JSON envelopes. Each envelope has a type field that identifies the widget and a data payload with the updated values. Messages arrive at varying intervals depending on the data source (KPIs every 5s, alerts on-demand, pipeline metrics every 2s).

type.description: Envelope type discriminator. Determines which widget should consume the data.

data.description: Payload shape varies by type. See examples below.

timestamp.description: Server timestamp when the envelope was emitted.

examples.label: system_health
examples.description: System KPIs: threats, monitored devices, uptime, response time.

examples.label: alerts
examples.description: Pushed when a new alert is raised or an existing alert changes status.

errors.description: Sent when the backend event bus encounters an unrecoverable error.
Sent when the server is shutting down gracefully.

connectionLifecycle.handshake: Standard HTTP upgrade to WebSocket. No authentication required in development; production requires a valid session cookie.
connectionLifecycle.heartbeat: Server sends a WebSocket ping frame every 30 seconds. Client must respond with pong within 10 seconds or the connection is closed.
connectionLifecycle.reconnection: Clients should reconnect with exponential backoff (1s initial, 30s max). On reconnect, the server immediately pushes a full state snapshot for all widget types.
connectionLifecycle.idleTimeout: No idle timeout — the server continuously pushes updates.
